program_info.title: Master of Science Thesis Degree Requirements and Program Timeline

coursework.description: Four courses, one from each category: Design, Implementation, Phenomena, Evaluation.
Any courses from the ISU Course Catalog.

coursework.requirement_details: Required for students entering Spring 2022 and after. Encouraged but not required for current students before that.

program_timeline.title: COMMITTEE MEMBERSHIP
program_timeline.description: Establish Academic Plan Committee (APC) of graduate faculty.
program_timeline.timing: As early as possible (recommended during second semester).
program_timeline.constraints: Must be approved BEFORE academic plan review.
program_timeline.warning: Do not submit Academic Plan until committee explains are approved.

program_timeline.title: ACADEMIC PLAN
program_timeline.description: List of courses taken and planned.
program_timeline.instructions_location: Graduate College website > Academic Plan (POSC Replacement)
program_timeline.warning: Do not apply for program completion until academic plan is approved.

program_timeline.title: APPLY FOR PROGRAM COMPLETION
program_timeline.description: Apply for the intended semester of graduation.
program_timeline.checks: Check Workday for I, N, NP, or F grades. Cannot graduate with unresolved grades.

program_timeline.title: FORMAT CHECK
program_timeline.description: Review Thesis/Dissertation Format Requirements or meet with Center for Communication Excellence (CCE).

program_timeline.title: FINAL ORAL EXAM REQUEST
program_timeline.timing: Must be submitted three weeks prior to exam date.

program_timeline.title: OPEN THESIS ACCOUNT WITH ProQUEST
program_timeline.description: Input thesis title for Commencement Program.
program_timeline.timing: No later than the deadline date.

scholarly_document_fee.name: Scholarly Document Fee
scholarly_document_fee.details: One-time fee charged to U-bill for students submitting dissertation/thesis/creative component.

program_timeline.title: GRADUATE APPROVAL FORM and RESULTS OF FINAL ORAL EXAM
program_timeline.description: Major professor(s) and DOGE(s) review/approve final dissertation content and format.
program_timeline.instructions_location: Graduate College website > Oral Examinations > Graduate Approval Form

program_timeline.title: UPLOAD AND SUBMIT FINAL THESIS to ProQUEST/UMI
program_timeline.constraints: Must be approved by Graduate College before graduation.

program_of_study_committee.timeline: Appointed before the end of the first year.

ms_thesis.members: At least 3 members of graduate faculty
requirements: Two HCI faculty members (including major professor)
One member outside student's major (not HCI faculty)
ms_thesis.note: Result of focused research effort prepared according to Graduate College requirements.

requirements: One HCI faculty member as major professor

program_of_study_committee.instructions_location: Graduate College Website > Academic Plan Committee

academic_plan_details.definition: List of courses taken or planned to meet degree requirements.
academic_plan_details.timing: Develop early, submit in Workday during second semester.
academic_plan_details.process: Student and major professor develop AP with APC approval.
academic_plan_details.changes: Approved in Workday by Graduate College, major professor, committee members, and DOGE.
academic_plan_details.instructions_location: Workday Student > Completing your Academic Plan (POSC Replacement)

process: Non-confidential answers sent to advisor/major professor for sign-off
Confidential answers stay with HCI DOGE

expired_courses_policy.definition: Time between year credits were earned and year of graduation.
expired_courses_policy.limit: Graduate College limit is 7 years (Time-to-Degree Limit).
expired_courses_policy.program_policy: The HCI Graduate Program accepts expired courses on an HCI student's academic plan.
